flashcards.back: An ADT that supports inserting elements with priorities and extracting either the minimum (min-PQ) or the maximum (max-PQ) element efficiently. Typically implemented using a binary heap with O(log n) insert and delete.

An ADT that supports inserting elements and efficiently extracting both the minimum and the maximum elements. Key operations: insert, deleteMin, deleteMax, getMin, getMax. Can be implemented using min-max heaps or interval heaps.

A complete binary tree where levels alternate between min-levels (even) and max-levels (odd). The root is at a min-level. The minimum is at the root; the maximum is among the children of the root. Supports all DEPQ operations in O(log n).
For every node i (except the root), the value of i is less than or equal to the value of its parent: A[parent(i)] >= A[i]. This ensures the maximum element is always at the root.

A complete binary tree where each node stores a closed interval [a, b] with a <= b. The left endpoints form a min-heap and the right endpoints form a max-heap. Used to implement DEPQs with O(log n) operations.
